paragraphs.text:
Yes! There are many ligands in the SRD-46 database with more than 6 reported pKa values. These multiple entries reflect measurements under different conditions (temperature, ionic strength, electrolyte). Here are the **top 20 ligands ranked by number of reported pKa measurements**:

| Rank | Ligand ID | Ligand Name (Common Name) | # pKa Values |
|------|-----------|---------------------------|:------------:|
| 1 | ligand_8412 | Nitrilotris(methylenephosphonic acid) (NTP) | 42 |
| 2 | ligand_6356 | Diethylenetrinitrilopentaacetic acid (DTPA) | 38 |
| 3 | ligand_6277 | Ethylenedinitrilotetraacetic acid (EDTA) | 36 |
| 4 | ligand_8380 | Iminobis(methylenephosphonic acid) (IDP) | 28 |
| 5 | ligand_7246 | 1,4,7,10,13,16-Hexaazahexadecane (Pentaethylenehexamine) | 24 |
| 6 | ligand_6165 | Nitrilotriacetic acid (NTA) | 22 |
| 7 | ligand_10117 | Hydrogen triphosphate (Triphosphoric acid) | 22 |
| 8 | ligand_10113 | Hydrogen phosphate (Phosphoric acid) | 21 |
| 9 | ligand_9142 | 1-Hydroxyethane-1,1-diphosphonic acid (Etidronic acid) | 21 |
| 10 | ligand_10114 | Hydrogen diphosphate (Pyrophosphoric acid) | 20 |
| 11 | ligand_5937 | N-(Phosphonomethyl)glycine (Glyphosate) | 20 |
| 12 | ligand_7244 | 1,4,7,10,13-Pentaazatridecane (Tetraethylenepentamine) | 18 |
| 13 | ligand_8907 | Butanedioic acid (Succinic acid) | 16 |
| 14 | ligand_8873 | Propanedioic acid (Malonic acid) | 16 |
| 15 | ligand_8872 | Ethanedioic acid (Oxalic acid) | 15 |
| 16 | ligand_5760 | Aminoacetic acid (Glycine) | 15 |
| 17 | ligand_9058 | 2-Hydroxypropane-1,2,3-tricarboxylic acid (Citric acid) | 14 |
| 18 | ligand_5898 | L-2-Amino-3-(4-imidazolyl)propanoic acid (Histidine) | 14 |
| 19 | ligand_10096 | Hydrogen carbonate (Carbonic acid) | 13 |
| 20 | ligand_10077 | Hydrogen vanadate (Vanadic acid) | 13 |

paragraphs.text:
### Key Insights

- **NTP leads with 42 pKa entries** — this aminophosphonate has 6 distinct protonation steps (H₆L), and each step has been measured under many different ionic strength and temperature conditions, generating numerous entries.
- **DTPA (38 entries)** has up to **8 distinct protonation species** (L through H₈L), making it the ligand with the most distinct protonation steps in the database. This reflects its five carboxylate groups and three amine nitrogens.
- **EDTA (36 entries)** is one of the most extensively studied chelators, with 6 distinct protonation species measured across a wide range of conditions (5–41°C, ionic strengths up to 3 M).
- The high counts for simpler acids like succinic, malonic, and oxalic acid (which have only 2 pKa steps each) arise because these fundamental ligands have been measured repeatedly under many different experimental conditions.

**Important distinction:** The counts above represent total reported pKa *measurements* (across varying conditions), not necessarily distinct protonation steps. In terms of **distinct protonation steps**, DTPA leads with 8 (L → H₈L), followed by NTP and pentaethylenehexamine with 6 each, and EDTA with 6 as well.

These large numbers of reported pKa values underscore the importance of these ligands in coordination chemistry, environmental science, and industrial applications — and the database's thorough coverage of condition-dependent equilibrium data.

*Source: NIST SRD-46 (Critically Selected Stability Constants of Metal Complexes)*
